general_info.definition: Highly blue luminescent carbon dots (CDs) introduced to synthesize CDs embedded metal-organic framework@ molecularly imprinted polymer nanoparticles (CDs@ZIF-8@MIP) acting as signal transducer and detectable fluorescence signals

method.conditions: Reaction of 10mL AEAPMS and 0.5g anhydrous citric acid at 240°C for 1min

applications.performance: Fluorescence presented a well linear decline with the increasing concentration of QCT from 0 0-50.0 µM; Limit of Detection (LOD): 2.9 nM (S/N=3); Precision for 0.5 µM QCT: 1.9% (n=11). Quenching follows a dynamic process via inner filter effect. Adsorption equilibrium time: 15 min (vs 210 min for CDs@MIP without ZIF-8).
applications.comparative_advantage: Superior to CDs@MIP (without ZIF-8): 1.8× higher adsorption capacity and faster equilibrium (15 min vs 210 min). High selectivity for QCT over structural analogs (isorhamnetin, epicatechin, daidzein, rutin). Validated in real Ginkgo biloba extract capsules with results matching HPLC.
